content: Healthcare financing models are categorized into four main types: (1) Bismarck model - social health insurance funded by employer/employee payroll contributions, with multiple non-profit sickness funds (Germany, France, Japan); (2) Beveridge model - tax-funded national health service with government provision (UK, Spain, Nordic countries); (3) National Health Insurance - single-payer combining Beveridge funding with private delivery (Canada, South Korea, Taiwan); (4) Out-of-pocket model - no organized financing, patients pay directly (most low-income countries). Each model has distinct implications for equity, efficiency, and access.
